name: Mobile Access Plan — WebAuthn PWA + Capability Ledger
outcome_goal: Nate can approve or revoke Praxis autonomous-agent work from a WebAuthn-backed PWA; every autonomous action that reaches control_commands.py is judged against a stateful capability grant in Praxis.db (scope, budget, expiry, plan_hash). The old verb-level _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES authority is gone; it survives only as a UI risk hint. A rogue workflow that chains local edit -> test -> commit -> push cannot escape the grant envelope. Layered on top of the grant envelope, a session-scoped blast-radius tracker (files_mutated, bytes_written, external_calls, spend_cents) gates even in-scope plans when thresholds are crossed. Runtime actions that violate an active operator_decisions policy emit policy_drift_events; autonomy_chain_ledger records the longest chain of auto-approved actions since last human tap. Both signals plus active blast-radius render on the mobile BurnRateHeader as the glance-level oversight primitive.
anti_requirements: Do NOT roll custom ed25519 HTTP signatures. Auth is WebAuthn Passkey (FIDO2 L3) via py_webauthn; bootstrap uses a single-use enrollment token only.
Do NOT ship a native iOS/Android app. The mobile control surface is a PWA served from the same host as Moon; instant deploys, no App Store gate.
Do NOT introduce an external policy engine (OPA/Rego/Cedar). Authority lives in Praxis.db tables (capability_grants, gate_evaluations). Policy is Python code reading those rows.
Do NOT replace the existing gate_evaluations table. Extend it (add grant_ref, plan_envelope_hash columns) so we keep every historical gate receipt.
Do NOT delete _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES blindly. Rename it to _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES and demote its role to surface rendering only. The authority path goes through resolve_capability_grant().
Do NOT auto-execute any command (autorun_ok flags, classify_control_intent AUTO_EXECUTE) unless an active grant covers it. Reads that feed destructive writes count.
Do NOT widen writes outside Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/, Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/, Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/, and Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/.
Do NOT change the public API of chat_orchestrator submission callers; the plan-envelope stamp is an additive pre-flight, not a signature change.

Do NOT add new mobile metric panels beyond the five proposed (Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System) plus the three-line always-visible header (pending approvals, burn rate, active-session blast-radius). policy_drift_count_24h and autonomy_depth land as two rows inside the existing Quality panel — NOT as new panels. Collapse and simplify — no comfort-food surfaces.
notes: Grounding: debate run workflow_bf339d4a5629 (positions B, C) + continuation workflow_e2f4e3d241b1 (position A defender + synthesis). See artifacts/workflow_outputs/workflow_output_workflow_e2f4e3d241b1_job_389_crossfire.synthesis.md for the verdict matrix. This spec encodes the converged HYBRID: WebAuthn (B) + PWA (B) + plan-hash envelope at submission (C + A defender) + extend gate_evaluations (A defender) + kill _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES as authority (C) while keeping it as UI hint (A). A second debate (debate_mobile_access_architecture_20260420) stress-tested this plan and added Pillars 3 + 4: migration 189_oversight_instrumentation.sql is already applied (adds policy_drift_events, autonomy_chain_ledger, session_blast_radius, session_blast_radius_policy). Three new jobs (wave2_blast_radius_tracker, wave2_policy_drift_detector, wave5_oversight_signals_in_metrics) wire the runtime and mobile surfaces to those tables.

jobs.label: wave0_integrated_design
jobs.description: Produce the integrated design doc that waves 1-6 consume. Names every table, endpoint, module, and contract type so downstream jobs have a single source of truth.
jobs.outcome_goal: A written design (committed under docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md) names: (a) every new/modified table and migration number, (b) every HTTP endpoint with path + method + auth requirement, (c) every Python module to be created, (d) every TypeScript module to be created under surfaces/app/src/mobile/, (e) the exact call graph from chat_orchestrator.request_workflow_submit_command through plan-envelope stamp -> resolve_capability_grant -> classify_control_intent -> gate_evaluations insert.
jobs.prompt:
Write the integrated design doc at docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md.

Read first:
- artifacts/workflow_outputs/workflow_output_workflow_e2f4e3d241b1_job_389_crossfire.synthesis.md (verdicts)
- artifacts/workflow_outputs/workflow_output_workflow_e2f4e3d241b1_job_388_position.a.defender.retry.md (hybrid nuance)
- artifacts/workflow_outputs/workflow_output_workflow_bf339d4a5629_job_386_position.c.gate.supremacy.replacement.md (capability scopes)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/control_commands.py (lines 423-470, 822-860 choke point)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/submission_policy.py (existing PolicyEngine.evaluate_gate path)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_tools.py (chat_orchestrator submission path)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/self_healing.py (FIX_AND_RETRY, RETRY_ESCALATED)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py (autorun_ok)
- Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/003_gate_and_promotion_policy.sql (existing gate_evaluations schema)

The doc must contain these sections and nothing else:

1. THE HYBRID — one paragraph restating: WebAuthn (B) + PWA (B) + plan-hash envelope at submission (C + A) + extend gate_evaluations not replace (A) + kill _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES as authority but keep as UI hint (C + A).

2. TABLES — for each new/modified table, the CREATE/ALTER statement in SQL, the owning migration file name, and which runtime module reads/writes it:
   - 185_mobile_capability_ledger.sql: capability_grants (grant_id PK, issued_by, subject_run_id NULL, subject_job_id NULL, plan_hash, allowed_operations TEXT[], write_scope TEXT[], read_scope TEXT[], budget_ceiling_cents, budget_consumed_cents, expires_at, issued_at, revoked_at NULL, device_enrollment_id FK, signature); device_enrollments (enrollment_id PK, public_key_cose BYTEA, credential_id BYTEA UNIQUE, aaguid, sign_count BIGINT, created_at, revoked_at NULL, label); approval_requests (request_id PK, run_id, job_label, intent_kind, intent_payload JSONB, proposed_plan_envelope JSONB, proposed_plan_hash, status CHECK IN ('pending','ratified','revoked','expired'), created_at, decided_at NULL, decided_by NULL, resulting_grant_id FK NULL).
   - 186_gate_evaluations_grant_coverage.sql: ALTER TABLE gate_evaluations ADD COLUMN grant_ref TEXT NULL REFERENCES capability_grants(grant_id); ADD COLUMN plan_envelope_hash TEXT NULL; index (grant_ref, decided_at DESC).
   - 187_webauthn_challenges.sql: webauthn_challenges (challenge_id PK, challenge BYTEA, purpose CHECK IN ('register','assert','step_up'), associated_enrollment_id NULL, created_at, expires_at, consumed_at NULL).

3. HTTP ENDPOINTS — for each, path + method + auth requirement + request/response schema sketch. Must cover: POST /auth/webauthn/register/begin, POST /auth/webauthn/register/complete, POST /auth/webauthn/assert/begin, POST /auth/webauthn/assert/complete, POST /auth/bootstrap/exchange (single-use token -> enrollment challenge), POST /approvals/request, GET /approvals/pending, POST /approvals/{request_id}/ratify (requires WebAuthn step-up), POST /approvals/{request_id}/revoke, GET /approvals/subscribe (SSE), POST /capability/grants/{grant_id}/revoke, GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot (returns the 5 panels + 2 header lines).

4. PYTHON MODULES — names and responsibilities: Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/types.py (CapabilityGrant, PlanEnvelope, DeviceEnrollment, ApprovalRequest dataclasses), runtime/capability/resolver.py (resolve_capability_grant), runtime/capability/plan_envelope.py (compute_plan_hash, stamp_plan_envelope), runtime/capability/approval_lifecycle.py (open_approval_request, ratify_approval, revoke_grant), runtime/webauthn/ (register, assert, step_up ceremonies using py_webauthn), surfaces/api/handlers/webauthn_auth.py, surfaces/api/handlers/approvals.py, surfaces/api/handlers/mobile_metrics.py.

5. TYPESCRIPT MODULES — surfaces/app/src/mobile/: main.tsx (router), service-worker.ts, manifest.json, pages/Canvas.tsx (shell), pages/ApprovalInbox.tsx, pages/FivePanels.tsx, components/PendingBadge.tsx, components/BurnRateHeader.tsx, components/PlanEnvelopePreview.tsx, api/webauthn.ts (navigator.credentials), api/approvals.ts, api/sse.ts.

6. THE CALL GRAPH — a numbered trace of what happens when Claude calls chat_orchestrator.request_workflow_submit_command for a workflow that mutates files:
   (1) chat_tools.py builds ControlIntent
   (2) NEW: plan_envelope.stamp_plan_envelope(intent) computes plan_hash from (spec_path, allowed_ops, write_scope, estimated_spend)
   (3) NEW: resolver.resolve_capability_grant(intent, plan_hash, requested_by_ref) queries capability_grants WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND expires_at > now() AND plan_hash = intent.plan_hash AND intent.command_type = ANY(allowed_operations)
   (4) if covered -> control_commands.classify_control_intent returns AUTO_EXECUTE, records gate_evaluations row with grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash
   (5) if not covered -> approval_lifecycle.open_approval_request inserts into approval_requests with status='pending' and returns GATE; SSE fires to /approvals/subscribe; PWA badge increments
   (6) Nate taps ratify in PWA -> WebAuthn step-up assertion -> POST /approvals/{id}/ratify -> ratify_approval issues a new capability_grants row bound to this plan_hash -> gate_evaluations row written with decision='accept' + grant_ref
   (7) the executor retries classify_control_intent which now returns AUTO_EXECUTE

7. WHERE _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES GOES — rename to _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES at control_commands.py:455, remove from the classify_control_intent authority check at :825, expose through a new get_ui_risk_hint(intent) helper that the approval inbox uses to sort/color items. Reference the rename in the doc and mention that tests at tests/unit/test_control_commands.py that assert on AUTO_EXECUTE for workflow.submit must be updated to also require an active grant fixture.

8. STOLEN-PHONE DEFENSE — grant issuance requires WebAuthn step-up assertion within 30s (freshness window recorded in approval_requests.decided_at vs. challenge.created_at). Revocation is one tap from the PWA home header. Device enrollments can be revoked from Praxis CLI: praxis workflow capability revoke-device --label <label>.

Do not write any code. Produce the doc, commit it, and print the full path + section outline.
jobs.verify_command: test -f docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md && grep -q 'capability_grants' docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md && grep -q '_UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES' docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md

jobs.label: wave0_migration_capability_ledger
jobs.description: Write migrations 180, 181, 182 per the design doc.
jobs.outcome_goal: Three migration files exist and apply cleanly against the dev Postgres. Tables capability_grants, device_enrollments, approval_requests, webauthn_challenges are created; gate_evaluations has grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash columns + supporting index.
jobs.prompt:
Write the three migrations exactly as specified in docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md section 2.

1. Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/185_mobile_capability_ledger.sql — capability_grants, device_enrollments, approval_requests (with all columns, constraints, indexes named in the design doc).
2. Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/186_gate_evaluations_grant_coverage.sql — ALTER TABLE gate_evaluations ADD COLUMN grant_ref TEXT NULL REFERENCES capability_grants(grant_id); ADD COLUMN plan_envelope_hash TEXT NULL; CREATE INDEX gate_evaluations_grant_ref_decided_at_idx ON gate_evaluations (grant_ref, decided_at DESC) WHERE grant_ref IS NOT NULL.
3. Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/187_webauthn_challenges.sql — webauthn_challenges table + expiry index.

Constraints:
- Idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS).
- Every CHECK constraint named (capability_grants_budget_ceiling_positive, approval_requests_status_valid, webauthn_challenges_purpose_valid).
- ON DELETE RESTRICT foreign keys — never cascade grant history.
- Add COMMENT ON TABLE for each new table explaining its authority role.
- Register the migrations in system_authority/workflow_migration_authority.json per the existing pattern.

Verify by running psql -f on each file against postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis.
jobs.verify_command: psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -f 'Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/185_mobile_capability_ledger.sql' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -f 'Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/186_gate_evaluations_grant_coverage.sql' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -f 'Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/187_webauthn_challenges.sql' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c '\d capability_grants' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c '\d approval_requests' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c '\d device_enrollments' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c '\d webauthn_challenges' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c 'SELECT grant_ref, plan_envelope_hash FROM gate_evaluations LIMIT 0'

jobs.label: wave0_contract_types
jobs.description: Define the Python dataclasses that waves 2-4 pass around.
jobs.outcome_goal: Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/types.py defines CapabilityGrant, PlanEnvelope, DeviceEnrollment, ApprovalRequest as frozen dataclasses with from_row / to_row helpers; types re-exported from runtime/capability/__init__.py.
jobs.prompt:
Create Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/ as a new package. Add:
- __init__.py exporting the dataclasses.
- types.py with @dataclass(frozen=True) definitions mirroring the table columns exactly:
  - CapabilityGrant: grant_id, issued_by, subject_run_id, subject_job_id, plan_hash, allowed_operations (tuple[str,...]), write_scope (tuple[str,...]), read_scope (tuple[str,...]), budget_ceiling_cents, budget_consumed_cents, expires_at (datetime), issued_at, revoked_at (datetime | None), device_enrollment_id, signature.
  - PlanEnvelope: plan_hash, spec_path, allowed_operations, write_scope, read_scope, estimated_spend_cents, computed_at. Include classmethod from_intent(intent) that canonicalizes and hashes (sha256 over a JSON-canonical form).
  - DeviceEnrollment: enrollment_id, public_key_cose (bytes), credential_id (bytes), aaguid, sign_count, created_at, revoked_at, label.
  - ApprovalRequest: request_id, run_id, job_label, intent_kind, intent_payload (Mapping[str, Any]), proposed_plan_envelope (PlanEnvelope), proposed_plan_hash, status (Literal['pending','ratified','revoked','expired']), created_at, decided_at, decided_by, resulting_grant_id.

Also add runtime/capability/errors.py with: CapabilityExhaustedError, GrantNotCoveringIntentError, GrantExpiredError, StepUpRequiredError.

No SQL in this file. Pure types. Unit test at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_capability_types.py covering plan_hash determinism (same intent -> same hash, different intent -> different hash) and round-trip to_row / from_row for each dataclass.

jobs.label: wave1_webauthn_endpoints
jobs.description: Implement the WebAuthn register/assert/step-up ceremonies using py_webauthn and wire them into surfaces/api/.
jobs.outcome_goal: A browser running at localhost can call /auth/webauthn/register/begin, complete registration with a software authenticator, then call /auth/webauthn/assert/begin and /auth/webauthn/assert/complete to produce a session-bound assertion. All challenges are stored in webauthn_challenges and consumed on completion.
jobs.prompt:
Install py_webauthn (add to requirements) and implement the WebAuthn ceremony as a standalone module.

Files:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/webauthn/__init__.py
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/webauthn/ceremonies.py — generate_registration_options, verify_registration_response, generate_authentication_options, verify_authentication_response (wrappers around py_webauthn that also persist/consume challenges via storage/postgres/webauthn_repository.py).
- Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/webauthn_repository.py — PostgresWebauthnRepository with insert_challenge, consume_challenge, insert_enrollment, get_enrollment_by_credential_id, bump_sign_count, revoke_enrollment.
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/webauthn_auth.py — aiohttp handlers for /auth/webauthn/register/begin, /auth/webauthn/register/complete, /auth/webauthn/assert/begin, /auth/webauthn/assert/complete, /auth/bootstrap/exchange (single-use 5-min enrollment token in keychain).
- Wire handlers into Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/rest.py at the existing handler registration site (find it by locating other /auth/ routes).

Constraints:
- RP ID is configurable via env MOBILE_RP_ID (default 'localhost' for dev).
- No bespoke signature scheme. No ed25519 hand-rolled. Only py_webauthn's verified ceremony.
- Bootstrap enrollment token is single-use and 5-min TTL; stored hashed in webauthn_challenges (purpose='register') not in plaintext.
- Step-up assertion for sensitive operations is just a new /auth/webauthn/assert/begin flow with purpose='step_up' and a 30-second expiry.
- Tests at tests/integration/test_webauthn_ceremony.py using py_webauthn's test fixtures (it ships with an in-memory software authenticator).

jobs.label: wave1_session_middleware
jobs.description: Add a session middleware that binds each /mobile/* and /approvals/* request to a verified WebAuthn assertion.
jobs.outcome_goal: Any /mobile/* or /approvals/* route without a valid, non-expired session cookie bound to a device_enrollments row returns 401. Unauthenticated bootstrap path remains open.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/_mobile_session.py with a middleware that:
1. Reads an httpOnly, SameSite=Strict session cookie 'praxis_mobile_session'.
2. Looks up the session in a new mobile_sessions table (migration 188_mobile_sessions.sql: session_id PK, enrollment_id FK, issued_at, expires_at, revoked_at). Sessions are issued only after a successful verify_authentication_response in handlers/webauthn_auth.py.
3. Rejects requests where enrollment is revoked, session is expired, or cookie is missing.
4. Attaches authed enrollment_id to request state for downstream handlers.
5. For ratify/revoke endpoints, additionally requires the request body to carry a step-up assertion that was produced within the last 30 seconds (challenge.created_at vs. now).

Register the middleware on the mobile route group in rest.py. Explicit allowlist: /auth/bootstrap/exchange and /auth/webauthn/* remain open.

Tests in tests/integration/test_mobile_session.py: (a) missing cookie -> 401, (b) revoked enrollment -> 401, (c) valid session -> handler reached, (d) ratify without step-up -> 403, (e) ratify with stale step-up (>30s) -> 403.

jobs.label: wave2_plan_envelope_stamp
jobs.description: Stamp a plan_hash on every submission intent at the chat_orchestrator entry point.
jobs.outcome_goal: chat_tools.request_workflow_submit_command computes a PlanEnvelope and attaches plan_hash to the ControlIntent BEFORE classify_control_intent runs. The hash is deterministic across retries of the same plan.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/plan_envelope.py with:
- compute_plan_hash(spec_path, allowed_operations, write_scope, read_scope, estimated_spend_cents) -> str (sha256 hex over a JSON-canonical form; key order sorted, lists normalized).
- stamp_plan_envelope(intent: ControlIntent, *, spec_path, write_scope, read_scope, estimated_spend_cents) -> tuple[ControlIntent, PlanEnvelope] — returns an updated intent with plan_hash populated plus the envelope.

Modify ControlIntent at Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/control_commands.py to add optional plan_hash and plan_envelope fields (default None; the resolver will require them).

Modify Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_tools.py at request_workflow_submit_command (around line 461-467) to call stamp_plan_envelope BEFORE passing the intent to classify_control_intent. The spec_path is already known; write_scope / read_scope come from the workflow spec's per-job fields; estimated_spend_cents comes from lane_policy.estimate_spend.

Tests at tests/unit/test_plan_envelope.py:
- Determinism: same inputs -> same hash.
- Key-order independence: shuffled dict input -> same hash.
- Sensitivity: changing write_scope changes the hash.
- Integration stub: stamp_plan_envelope produces an intent with populated plan_hash.

jobs.label: wave2_capability_resolver
jobs.description: Implement the resolver that answers 'is this intent covered by an active grant?'
jobs.outcome_goal: resolve_capability_grant(intent, plan_hash, requested_by_ref) returns CoverageDecision (covered: bool, grant_id | None, reason_code). It queries capability_grants with fail-closed semantics: expired, revoked, over-budget, missing plan_hash match, or uncovered command type all return covered=False.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/resolver.py:

- CoverageDecision dataclass: covered (bool), grant_id (str | None), reason_code (str), remaining_budget_cents (int | None).
- resolve_capability_grant(conn, intent, plan_hash, requested_by_ref) -> CoverageDecision.
  Query: SELECT * FROM capability_grants WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND expires_at > now() AND plan_hash = %s AND %s = ANY(allowed_operations) ORDER BY expires_at DESC LIMIT 1.
  If no row: CoverageDecision(False, None, 'no_active_grant').
  If row.budget_consumed_cents + intent.estimated_spend_cents > row.budget_ceiling_cents: CoverageDecision(False, grant_id, 'budget_exhausted').
  If intent.write_scope not subset of row.write_scope: CoverageDecision(False, grant_id, 'write_scope_outside_grant').
  Else CoverageDecision(True, grant_id, 'covered', remaining_budget_cents).

- consume_grant_budget(conn, grant_id, spent_cents) — atomic UPDATE ... SET budget_consumed_cents = budget_consumed_cents + %s WHERE grant_id = %s AND budget_consumed_cents + %s <= budget_ceiling_cents; returns True if the UPDATE matched a row, else raises CapabilityExhaustedError.

Add Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/capability_repository.py with PostgresCapabilityRepository: insert_grant, get_active_grants_for_plan, revoke_grant, consume_budget.

Tests at tests/integration/test_capability_resolver.py using rollback-isolated conn:
- covered -> True with grant_id.
- expired grant -> False, 'no_active_grant'.
- revoked grant -> False.
- plan_hash mismatch -> False.
- budget exhausted -> False.
- write_scope outside grant -> False.
- consume_grant_budget races two concurrent consumes; second raises.

jobs.label: wave2_blast_radius_tracker
jobs.description: Runtime instrumentation for session_blast_radius + mid-plan gate (debate pillar 4).
jobs.outcome_goal: Every file mutation, external call, and spend event updates the active session_blast_radius row. When any dimension crosses its threshold in session_blast_radius_policy (seeded 'default' row: 50 files, 5 MiB, 20 external calls, $10), the runtime opens a mid-plan approval_request and writes session_blast_radius.gate_triggered_at + gate_triggered_dim — regardless of grant coverage. The mobile canvas can read the active row inline with burn rate.
jobs.prompt:
Migration 189_oversight_instrumentation.sql is already applied; the tables session_blast_radius and session_blast_radius_policy exist. This job wires the runtime.

Files to create:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/blast_radius.py — module with:
    - BlastRadiusSnapshot dataclass mirroring the table columns.
    - open_session(conn, run_id) -> session_id: INSERT a new row and set as the current active session.
    - record_mutation(conn, session_id, files_delta, bytes_delta): atomic UPDATE ... SET files_mutated = files_mutated + %s, bytes_written = bytes_written + %s, last_updated = now() WHERE session_id = %s AND closed_at IS NULL RETURNING *. After update, call check_thresholds.
    - record_external_call(conn, session_id, count_delta=1): similarly bumps external_calls.
    - record_spend(conn, session_id, spend_cents_delta): similarly bumps spend_cents.
    - check_thresholds(conn, row) -> ThresholdDecision: reads session_blast_radius_policy WHERE active=true, returns the first dimension exceeded or None.
    - trigger_gate(conn, session_id, triggered_dim, plan_envelope): sets gate_triggered_at/gate_triggered_dim, calls runtime.capability.approval_lifecycle.open_approval_request with intent_kind='blast_radius_threshold_crossed', returns the approval_requests.request_id so callers can propagate it to gate_approval_ref.
    - close_session(conn, session_id, reason): sets closed_at and closed_reason.
- Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/blast_radius_repository.py — thin CRUD.

Call sites to instrument (single-site hooks; avoid scattering):
1. runtime/control_commands.py — the main execute-intent path: after a successful mutation, call record_mutation with files_delta=1 and bytes_delta=len(new_content). Grep for the actual write helper.
2. adapters/provider_transport.py — every outbound HTTP call increments external_calls (record_external_call).
3. runtime/capability/resolver.py consume_grant_budget — after atomic spend consumption, also record_spend with the same spend_cents.

Integration tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_blast_radius_tracker.py using rollback-isolated conn:
- open_session starts counters at 0.
- record_mutation increments atomically (two concurrent calls both land).
- Crossing threshold_files_mutated fires trigger_gate; subsequent record_mutation calls still update counters but do NOT fire gate again (idempotent: gate_triggered_at stays at first trip).
- gate_triggered_dim records the specific dimension exceeded.
- close_session closes the row; reopening another session works.
- Default policy row is honored even if no policy_id is passed explicitly.

jobs.label: wave2_policy_drift_detector
jobs.description: Emit policy_drift_events whenever runtime attempts a violation of an active operator_decisions policy (debate pillar 3).
jobs.outcome_goal: Every path that was neutralized as part of the anthropic CLI-only cutover (task_assembler._call_haiku, provider_transport builtin fallback, docker_runner ANTHROPIC_API_KEY forwarding, agent_spawner _PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS_FALLBACK, _probe.anthropic_models_list) now additionally emits a policy_drift_events row tagged with policy_id='providers::anthropic', decision_ref='decision.2026-04-20.anthropic-cli-only-restored', detector_kind='anthropic_api_call_attempt' BEFORE returning its neutralized response. Future detectors plug into the same emit helper without changing callers.
jobs.prompt:
Migration 189_oversight_instrumentation.sql is already applied; policy_drift_events exists.

Files to create:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/policy_drift.py — module with:
    - emit_drift(conn, *, policy_id, decision_ref, detector_kind, violator_path, violator_run_id=None, action_summary, remediation_hint=None) -> drift_event_id: INSERT a row, returns the id. Cheap; never raises (log and swallow on insert failure so drift detection never blocks the neutralization path).
    - recent_drift_count(conn, hours=24) -> int: SELECT count(*) FROM policy_drift_events WHERE resolved_at IS NULL AND detected_at > now() - make_interval(hours => %s). Used by mobile metrics snapshot.
    - drift_events(conn, hours=24, limit=50) -> list[DriftEvent]: newest-first for debug surfaces.
    - resolve_drift(conn, drift_event_id, resolved_by): sets resolved_at and resolved_by.
- Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/policy_drift_repository.py — thin CRUD.

Call sites to instrument (each was neutralized by the anthropic CLI-only cutover and MUST now also emit drift):
1. runtime/task_assembler._call_haiku — was direct anthropic API, rerouted to OpenRouter. If any caller still somehow reaches the anthropic branch, emit drift and raise. Use violator_path='runtime/task_assembler._call_haiku'.
2. adapters/provider_transport.py BUILTIN fallback — the anthropic entry has no api_endpoint. If provider_transport constructs an anthropic client with an api_endpoint present (e.g. via operator override), emit drift before proceeding.
3. adapters/docker_runner.py — if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is ever added back to _PROVIDER_AUTH_ENV_CANDIDATES, emit drift at container spawn.
4. runtime/agent_spawner.py — same check on _PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS_FALLBACK.
5. registry/provider_onboarding/_probe.py — anthropic_models_list currently returns []. If operator pastes a real api_endpoint and it reaches HTTP, emit drift + return [].

The emitter is the record; the neutralization path still returns its safe value. Drift is observability, not enforcement.

Integration tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_policy_drift_detector.py using rollback-isolated conn:
- emit_drift creates a row with the expected policy_id/detector_kind.
- emit_drift on a DB-insert failure logs and returns None (does not raise).
- recent_drift_count returns 0 when all drifts are resolved, non-zero otherwise.
- Simulating a task_assembler._call_haiku misroute (monkeypatch the providers var back to 'anthropic') records one drift_event before raising.

jobs.label: wave2_autonomy_chain_ledger
jobs.description: Chain-length counter for auto-approved actions; human tap closes the chain (debate pillar 3 part 2).
jobs.outcome_goal: At most one active autonomy_chain_ledger row at any time. classify_control_intent bumps depth_counter on AUTO_EXECUTE. approval_lifecycle.ratify_approval_request closes the active chain (terminated_at=now(), termination_reason='human_tap') and opens a new one, resetting depth_counter to 0. Mobile canvas autonomy_depth reads the active row's depth_counter.
jobs.prompt:
Migration 189_oversight_instrumentation.sql is already applied; autonomy_chain_ledger exists with a singleton partial-unique index on active rows.

Files to create:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/autonomy_chain.py — module with:
    - get_or_open_active(conn) -> chain_id: SELECT the active row (terminated_at IS NULL) or INSERT one if none exists. The singleton unique index means two concurrent opens serialize cleanly — one wins, the other reads the winner's row.
    - bump_depth(conn, run_id=None): SELECT chain_id via get_or_open_active, then UPDATE ... SET depth_counter = depth_counter + 1, last_updated = now(), current_run_id = COALESCE(%s, current_run_id) WHERE chain_id = %s AND terminated_at IS NULL.
    - close_on_human_tap(conn, reason='human_tap'): UPDATE ... SET terminated_at = now(), last_human_tap_at = now(), termination_reason = %s WHERE terminated_at IS NULL. Then get_or_open_active opens a fresh chain at depth 0 on next bump.
    - current_depth(conn) -> int: SELECT depth_counter FROM autonomy_chain_ledger WHERE terminated_at IS NULL LIMIT 1; returns 0 if none.

Call-site hooks (exactly two — keep it surgical):
1. runtime/control_commands.classify_control_intent — when returning ControlPolicyDecision(mode=AUTO_EXECUTE, ...), call autonomy_chain.bump_depth(conn, run_id) before return.
2. runtime/capability/approval_lifecycle.ratify_approval_request — at the top of the atomic block, call autonomy_chain.close_on_human_tap(conn).

Integration tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_autonomy_chain_ledger.py using rollback-isolated conn:
- Empty table -> current_depth() == 0; bump_depth opens a chain and depth becomes 1.
- Consecutive bump_depth calls monotonically increase depth_counter.
- close_on_human_tap terminates the active chain; current_depth() == 0 immediately after.
- After a close, the next bump_depth opens a fresh chain at depth 1 (not continuing the old depth).
- The partial unique index prevents two active rows even under concurrent open_active attempts (simulate with two cursors).

jobs.label: wave3_command_bus_rewire
jobs.description: Rewire control_commands.classify_control_intent to use the capability resolver as the authority; demote _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES to a UI hint.
jobs.outcome_goal: The only path to AUTO_EXECUTE is through resolve_capability_grant returning covered=True. _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES is renamed to _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES and is never consulted by classify_control_intent. All existing callers of classify_control_intent now pass plan_hash.
jobs.prompt:
Rewire the authority path at Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/control_commands.py.

1. Rename _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES -> _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES at line 455. Add a docstring: 'UI hint only. NOT an authority. classify_control_intent MUST NOT route on this set. Used by approval inbox rendering to color/sort pending items.'

2. Rewrite classify_control_intent (line 822) signature to:
   def classify_control_intent(conn, intent, *, plan_hash, requested_by_ref) -> ControlPolicyDecision.
   Body:
   - If intent.plan_hash is None: raise ValueError('plan_hash required; call plan_envelope.stamp_plan_envelope before classify_control_intent').
   - decision = resolve_capability_grant(conn, intent, plan_hash, requested_by_ref).
   - If decision.covered: return ControlPolicyDecision(mode=AUTO_EXECUTE, risk_level=intent.risk_level, grant_ref=decision.grant_id, reason_code='grant_covers_intent').
   - Else: return ControlPolicyDecision(mode=GATE, risk_level=intent.risk_level, grant_ref=None, reason_code=decision.reason_code). The caller will open an approval_request.

3. Add get_ui_risk_hint(intent) -> Literal['auto','gate','threshold'] helper that reads _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES for PWA rendering. This is the ONLY remaining consumer of the renamed set.

4. Fix every caller of classify_control_intent to pass plan_hash + requested_by_ref. Grep for 'classify_control_intent' across Code&DBs/Workflow/ and update each site. If a site can't provide plan_hash (unstamped intent), that site must call stamp_plan_envelope first.

5. Extend ControlPolicyDecision to include grant_ref and reason_code. Update its to_dict.

6. Update existing tests at tests/unit/test_control_commands.py: any assertion that classify_control_intent returns AUTO_EXECUTE for a command must first create a capability_grants row covering that plan. Add a helper _issue_test_grant(conn, intent) under tests/unit/_capability_fixtures.py.

Verify _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES does not appear anywhere in the runtime after the rewrite:
ripgrep _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES Code&DBs/Workflow/ must return zero matches.

jobs.label: wave3_self_healing_rewire
jobs.description: Route FIX_AND_RETRY / RETRY_ESCALATED / SKIP recommendations through the capability resolver.
jobs.outcome_goal: self_healing.py cannot issue an autonomous repair action unless an active grant covers it. The RecoveryAction enum gains a reason_code field when a recommendation is blocked by grant coverage.
jobs.prompt:
Modify Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/self_healing.py around lines 125-130.

1. Add a gate_recovery_action(conn, recovery_action, run_id, plan_hash) helper that:
   - Builds a synthetic ControlIntent representing the recovery (command_type='workflow.retry' for FIX_AND_RETRY/RETRY_ESCALATED, 'workflow.skip_job' for SKIP, 'workflow.cancel' for HALT).
   - Calls resolve_capability_grant on it.
   - If not covered: downgrade the RecoveryAction to 'pending_approval' and insert an approval_requests row (via runtime.capability.approval_lifecycle.open_approval_request).

2. Every site that returns a RecoveryAction now goes through gate_recovery_action first.

3. Remove any autorun_ok implicit-permission pattern from self_healing.

4. Tests at tests/integration/test_self_healing_capability.py: given an active grant covering workflow.retry, FIX_AND_RETRY is returned as-is. Given no grant, FIX_AND_RETRY becomes pending_approval and a row appears in approval_requests.

jobs.label: wave3_remediation_rewire
jobs.description: Kill autorun_ok in data_dictionary_governance_remediation; route every remediation command through the resolver.
jobs.outcome_goal: grep autorun_ok Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py returns zero matches. Every GovernanceRemediationCommand now carries a grant_ref field populated by the resolver.
jobs.prompt:
Rewire Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py.

1. Remove the autorun_ok field from GovernanceRemediationCommand. Replace with grant_ref: str | None (None = requires approval).
2. Inside the builder path that currently sets autorun_ok=True at line 349, instead call resolve_capability_grant for the (command_type, plan_hash) pair. If covered, set grant_ref to the returned grant_id. If not, set grant_ref=None and flag the command as requiring approval.
3. Downstream consumers of GovernanceRemediationCommand (find with ripgrep) now check grant_ref is not None before executing; if None, they open an approval_requests row.
4. Update tests at tests/unit/test_data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py accordingly — remove autorun_ok assertions, add grant_ref assertions against fixture grants.

jobs.label: wave3_gate_evaluations_extension
jobs.description: Every gate_evaluations row now records grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash; existing submission_policy.py evaluate_gate path is extended, not replaced.
jobs.outcome_goal: Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/submission_policy.py passes grant_ref and plan_envelope_hash into engine.evaluate_gate, and _insert_gate_evaluation writes those two new columns. All new gate_evaluations rows have them populated; legacy rows stay NULL.
jobs.prompt:
Extend the existing gate evaluation path without reshaping it.

1. In Code&DBs/Workflow/policy/domain.py (or wherever engine.evaluate_gate lives), add grant_ref and plan_envelope_hash as optional kwargs on GateEvaluation and evaluate_gate.
2. In Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/submission_policy.py at line 137-154, read the active grant via resolve_capability_grant and pass grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash into engine.evaluate_gate.
3. Extend _insert_gate_evaluation (grep for it) to write the two new columns. Existing INSERT sites that do not have a grant (legacy bootstrap) pass NULL.
4. Tests at tests/integration/test_gate_and_promotion_policy.py: new row has grant_ref populated when a grant is active; block decision also records grant_ref so the denial is traceable.

jobs.label: wave4_approval_lifecycle
jobs.description: Build the approval_requests lifecycle: open -> pending -> ratified (issues grant) or revoked.
jobs.outcome_goal: runtime.capability.approval_lifecycle exposes open_approval_request, ratify_approval_request (issues a capability_grants row on success), revoke_approval_request, expire_stale_requests. Every state transition is atomic.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/approval_lifecycle.py with the full lifecycle.

- open_approval_request(conn, run_id, job_label, intent, plan_envelope) -> ApprovalRequest: inserts a row with status='pending', returns the row.
- ratify_approval_request(conn, request_id, ratified_by_enrollment_id, step_up_challenge_id) -> CapabilityGrant: atomically (a) verifies step_up_challenge_id was created <30s ago and is consumed here, (b) updates approval_requests SET status='ratified', decided_at=now(), decided_by=..., resulting_grant_id=NEW_ID WHERE status='pending', (c) inserts a capability_grants row with the plan envelope's allowed_operations/write_scope/read_scope/budget_ceiling, expires_at = now() + grant_duration (from intent or default 4 hours), device_enrollment_id=ratifier.
- revoke_approval_request(conn, request_id, revoked_by) -> ApprovalRequest: transition to 'revoked'; if resulting_grant_id already exists, also revoke that grant.
- revoke_grant(conn, grant_id, revoked_by) -> CapabilityGrant: UPDATE SET revoked_at=now().
- expire_stale_requests(conn, ttl_minutes=60) -> int: batch UPDATE for old pending rows.

Tests at tests/integration/test_approval_lifecycle.py using rollback-isolated conn for each case.

jobs.label: wave4_approval_endpoints
jobs.description: Expose the approval lifecycle over HTTP for the PWA.
jobs.outcome_goal: curl against POST /approvals/request, GET /approvals/pending, POST /approvals/{id}/ratify, POST /approvals/{id}/revoke works end-to-end given a valid mobile session cookie.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/approvals.py with aiohttp handlers. All routes require mobile session middleware (from wave1); ratify additionally requires step-up assertion in body.

- POST /approvals/request — called by the runtime (internal), inserts a pending row, responds with the request_id and an SSE notification.
- GET /approvals/pending — returns all status='pending' rows newest-first with the plan envelope summary.
- POST /approvals/{request_id}/ratify — body { step_up_challenge_id, step_up_assertion }; verifies step-up, then calls ratify_approval_request.
- POST /approvals/{request_id}/revoke — calls revoke_approval_request.
- POST /capability/grants/{grant_id}/revoke — calls revoke_grant.

Register in rest.py. Integration tests at tests/integration/test_approval_endpoints.py exercise each route.

jobs.label: wave4_sse_approvals_stream
jobs.description: Live-push approval changes to the PWA via SSE.
jobs.outcome_goal: GET /approvals/subscribe returns Server-Sent Events with 'pending_opened', 'ratified', 'revoked', 'expired' event types. The PWA can connect once and receive every change.
jobs.prompt:
Add an SSE endpoint /approvals/subscribe in Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/approvals.py.

- Keep a per-process subscriber set; emit events from open_approval_request / ratify_approval_request / revoke_* hooks (via a local pub-sub adapter — do not introduce Redis).
- Heartbeat every 15s.
- Clean up on client disconnect.
- Integration test at tests/integration/test_approvals_sse.py: open a subscriber, trigger open_approval_request, receive 'pending_opened' within 1s.

Do not over-engineer. In-process pub-sub is fine; we are single-user, single-VPS.

jobs.label: wave5_pwa_scaffold
jobs.description: Scaffold the mobile PWA under surfaces/app/src/mobile/ with router, manifest, service worker.
jobs.outcome_goal: Navigating a mobile breakpoint to /mobile loads a PWA shell installable from the browser menu; manifest.json declares name 'Praxis Control', black/white theme, single standalone window.
jobs.prompt:
Add the PWA scaffold under Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/.

- main.tsx — React router with routes /mobile, /mobile/approvals, /mobile/flow, /mobile/economy, /mobile/providers, /mobile/quality, /mobile/system, /mobile/enroll.
- manifest.json — name 'Praxis Control', short_name 'Praxis', display 'standalone', theme_color '#000000', background_color '#000000', icons pointing to /mobile/icons/*.
- service-worker.ts — registers, caches app shell + static assets only (no API caching); activates immediately on update (skipWaiting + clients.claim).
- api/client.ts — fetch wrapper that sends cookies (credentials: 'include') and parses JSON.
- api/webauthn.ts — thin wrapper over navigator.credentials.create/get using the server-issued challenge.
- styles/base.css — black background, white text, hollow circle nodes, subtle type-glow matching the Moon visual language.

Wire the mobile route group into surfaces/app/vite.config.ts so `npm --prefix 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app' run build` outputs both Moon and Mobile bundles.

Verify: npm --prefix 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app' run build succeeds and emits surfaces/app/dist/mobile/ with manifest.json and sw.js.
jobs.verify_command: cd 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app' && npm run build && test -f dist/mobile/manifest.json && test -f dist/mobile/sw.js

jobs.label: wave5_canvas_shell
jobs.description: Build the Moon-style canvas shell: always-visible header with pending approvals + burn rate; everything else compact-idle, expand-on-tap.
jobs.outcome_goal: Opening /mobile shows black canvas with white hollow nodes. Top two lines: 'Pending approvals: N' (badge, red if >0) and 'Burn rate: $X / $Y, expires HH:MM'. Five panel nodes below: Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System — each collapsed by default, expanding on tap.
jobs.prompt:
Build the canvas shell at Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/pages/Canvas.tsx and the shared header components.

- components/BurnRateHeader.tsx — polls GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot every 5s (or listens on SSE); renders two lines: pending approvals count with a badge (zero-state white, >0 red dot), and burn rate vs active-grant budget with expiry time HH:MM.
- components/PanelNode.tsx — a hollow circle node with title; compact-idle shows only the label; tap expands to inline content area.
- pages/Canvas.tsx — composes BurnRateHeader above a vertical stack of five PanelNodes; clicking a node expands it (no separate page).

Canvas rules (from Moon visual language memory):
- Black background #000, white nodes/text.
- Hollow nodes: 1px white border, no fill, subtle 2px glow matching type-glow.
- One view, one canvas. No tabs, no stacked navigation. Expansion is in-place.
- No breadcrumbs, no separate pages beyond the router entry points.

Visual test at surfaces/app/src/mobile/__tests__/canvas.visual.test.tsx using vitest + @testing-library/react: renders Canvas, asserts header renders, asserts five PanelNodes present, asserts tap expansion toggles content visibility.

jobs.label: wave5_approval_inbox
jobs.description: Build the Approval Inbox panel: pending list, plan-envelope preview, biometric ratify, revoke.
jobs.outcome_goal: Tapping the Pending Approvals badge expands into a list of pending approval_requests showing each plan envelope summary (command_type, write_scope, budget_ceiling, expires_at). Tapping Ratify triggers a WebAuthn step-up ceremony via the device's platform authenticator (fingerprint, face unlock, or device PIN — whatever the OS exposes); on success the grant is issued and the list updates via SSE.
jobs.prompt:
Build Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/pages/ApprovalInbox.tsx and supporting components.

- components/PlanEnvelopePreview.tsx — expandable card: command_type, spec_path, allowed_operations, write_scope, read_scope, estimated_spend, expires_in, UI risk hint from the server (auto/gate/threshold).
- components/RatifyButton.tsx — button that (1) fetches GET /auth/webauthn/assert/begin?purpose=step_up, (2) calls navigator.credentials.get, (3) POSTs to /approvals/{id}/ratify with the assertion + challenge_id. Shows success -> dismisses item. Shows failure with reason_code.
- components/RevokeButton.tsx — one-tap revoke.
- pages/ApprovalInbox.tsx — connects to SSE /approvals/subscribe, renders pending list newest-first; each item has PlanEnvelopePreview + RatifyButton + RevokeButton.

Tests at surfaces/app/src/mobile/__tests__/approval_inbox.test.tsx using mocked fetch and mocked navigator.credentials.

jobs.label: wave5_five_panels
jobs.description: Implement the five Moon-style metric panels: Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System.
jobs.outcome_goal: Each PanelNode expands into the correct dense one-view summary backed by GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot. No panel has more than ~6 numeric fields; no scrolling on the expanded view at standard mobile heights.
jobs.prompt:
Build the five panel pages plus the backing snapshot endpoint.

Backend: Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/mobile_metrics.py — GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot returns a compact JSON with keys: flow { in_flight, queued, planned, finished_24h, failed_24h, jobs_per_worker }, economy { spend_today_cents, spend_week_cents, budget_remaining_cents, burn_rate_cents_per_hour, http_429_count_1h }, providers { anthropic, openai, gemini, deepseek } each with { health, circuit_state, active_fallback }, quality { bugs_open, bugs_critical, self_heal_events_24h, friction_events_24h, pending_approvals }, system { worker_alive, heartbeat_lag_s, migrations_pending }.

Frontend: pages for each panel under surfaces/app/src/mobile/pages/panels/.
- FlowPanel.tsx, EconomyPanel.tsx, ProvidersPanel.tsx, QualityPanel.tsx, SystemPanel.tsx. Each is a compact grid of 4-6 key-value rows; no charts, no sparklines, no tables. Numeric-only.
- All panels poll /mobile/metrics/snapshot every 10s when visible.

Constraints:
- No panel introduces an additional metric beyond those listed. If a requested metric is missing from the snapshot endpoint, add it to the endpoint, not a second endpoint.
- Zero scrolling inside a panel at 844px viewport (standard modern-phone baseline; covers Pixel, Galaxy, and iPhone mid-range). If it would scroll, drop rows until it fits.

Integration test at tests/integration/test_mobile_metrics.py (backend) + surfaces/app/src/mobile/__tests__/panels.test.tsx (frontend).
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -m pytest --noconftest -q 'Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_mobile_metrics.py' && cd 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app' && npm test -- src/mobile/__tests__/panels.test.tsx

jobs.label: wave5_enroll_flow
jobs.description: Device enrollment flow from bootstrap token.
jobs.outcome_goal: Nate can (a) generate a single-use 5-min bootstrap token from CLI 'praxis workflow capability issue-bootstrap-token', (b) open /mobile/enroll on his phone, (c) paste the token, (d) register the device's platform authenticator (biometric or PIN) as a Passkey, and end up with a mobile session cookie that survives app restart.
jobs.prompt:
Build the device-enrollment flow.

CLI: add 'praxis workflow capability issue-bootstrap-token [--label <label>]' that calls an existing POST /auth/bootstrap/issue internal endpoint (internal means invokable only by the CLI process, not HTTP exposed publicly) which inserts a webauthn_challenges row with purpose='register' and returns the 32-byte token URL-encoded. Print the token and a QR-code-friendly URL: https://<rp_id>/mobile/enroll?token=<token>.

PWA: pages/Enroll.tsx at /mobile/enroll reads the token from the URL param or an input box, POSTs to /auth/bootstrap/exchange -> receives the register challenge, calls navigator.credentials.create, POSTs the attestation back to /auth/webauthn/register/complete. On success, server issues the mobile session cookie and redirects to /mobile.

Tests at tests/integration/test_enroll_flow.py: bootstrap token round-trip, expired token rejected, already-used token rejected, label stored.

jobs.label: wave5_oversight_signals_in_metrics
jobs.description: Surface policy_drift_count, autonomy_depth, and active-session blast-radius on the mobile canvas (debate pillars 3 + 4).
jobs.outcome_goal: GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot returns quality.policy_drift_count_24h, quality.autonomy_depth, and flow.blast_radius { files_mutated, bytes_written, external_calls, spend_cents, gate_triggered_dim }. BurnRateHeader renders a third line showing active-session blast-radius compactly ('47 files / 12 calls / $3.20'); when gate_triggered_dim is non-null the header line turns red. A non-zero policy_drift_count_24h adds a red dot to the pending-approvals badge even when there are zero pending approvals — because drift is itself a fact that needs human attention.
jobs.prompt:
Extend the existing metrics pipeline — no new endpoints, no new pages. All new signals land on GET /mobile/metrics/snapshot and the already-shipped BurnRateHeader.

Backend (Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/mobile_metrics.py):
1. Extend the snapshot response:
   - quality.policy_drift_count_24h: runtime.capability.policy_drift.recent_drift_count(conn, hours=24).
   - quality.autonomy_depth: runtime.capability.autonomy_chain.current_depth(conn).
   - flow.blast_radius: the active session_blast_radius row as a compact dict {session_id, files_mutated, bytes_written, external_calls, spend_cents, gate_triggered_dim} or null if no active session.
2. Do NOT add new metric keys beyond these. The spec's minimalism constraint holds.

Frontend:
- components/BurnRateHeader.tsx — add a third compact line below the existing two. Format: '{files} files / {calls} calls / ${spend}'. When gate_triggered_dim is non-null, render the whole line in red and prepend '⚠ '. When no active session, omit the line entirely (no empty placeholder).
- components/PendingBadge.tsx — adjust so the red dot also appears when policy_drift_count_24h > 0, independent of pending approval count.
- pages/panels/QualityPanel.tsx — add two rows: 'Policy drift 24h' and 'Autonomy depth'.

Tests:
- Backend at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_mobile_metrics_oversight.py: asserts the snapshot includes the new keys; injects a fixture drift event + fixture active chain at depth 5 + fixture active session with 30 files_mutated; asserts values round-trip.
- Frontend at Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/mobile/__tests__/oversight_header.test.tsx: snapshots BurnRateHeader in three states (no active session; active session under threshold; gate_triggered_dim='files_mutated').
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -m pytest --noconftest -q 'Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_mobile_metrics_oversight.py' && cd 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app' && npm test -- src/mobile/__tests__/oversight_header.test.tsx

jobs.label: wave6_stolen_phone_defense
jobs.description: Harden against stolen-unlocked-phone: step-up freshness, per-device revoke CLI, visible burn-rate ceiling.
jobs.outcome_goal: A stolen unlocked phone cannot ratify a new approval after 30s without re-biometric, cannot exceed the active grant budget, and can be revoked in one CLI command from Nate's desktop.
jobs.prompt:
Three specific defenses:

1. Step-up freshness: in POST /approvals/{id}/ratify, reject if step_up_challenge.created_at > 30s ago; verified in tests.

2. Per-device revoke: add 'praxis workflow capability revoke-device --label <label>' CLI that marks device_enrollments.revoked_at=now(), invalidates all mobile_sessions for that enrollment, and revokes all active capability_grants with device_enrollment_id=<enrollment>. Write the CLI plus an integration test at tests/integration/test_device_revoke.py.

3. Visible budget burn-rate: BurnRateHeader.tsx already shows 'Burn rate: $X / $Y'; add a hard visual change when burn_rate_cents_per_hour projects to exceed budget_ceiling before expires_at (panel turns red-border + burn rate displayed in red). This is the human-visible signal that autonomy is running hot.

Do not invent new tables. All three defenses are thin additions to existing code.

jobs.label: wave6_e2e_chain_attack
jobs.description: End-to-end tests for the chain attacks the debate identified: scope-widen, remediation-bypass, in-scope blast-radius, and policy-drift.
jobs.outcome_goal: Four E2E integration tests prove the attack paths are stopped: (a) workflow edits a file outside its grant's write_scope -> gate; (b) workflow attempts FIX_AND_RETRY without a covering grant -> approval_request opened; (c) in-scope plan crossing session_blast_radius_policy threshold -> gate regardless of grant coverage; (d) runtime attempting api.anthropic.com call emits a policy_drift_events row and surfaces on mobile metrics.
jobs.prompt:
Write end-to-end integration tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_chain_attack_defense.py.

Test 1 — commit-plus-push chain:
- Enroll a fixture device.
- Issue a grant covering {allowed_operations: ['workflow.submit', 'git.add', 'git.commit'], write_scope: ['docs/']}. Note: NOT git.push, NOT write_scope of Code&DBs/.
- Simulate an intent sequence: edit docs/foo.md (covered), git.commit (covered), edit Code&DBs/runtime/control_commands.py (NOT covered — write_scope mismatch), assert that classify_control_intent returns GATE with reason_code='write_scope_outside_grant'.
- Assert a row in approval_requests with proposed_plan_envelope that includes Code&DBs/ in its write_scope (so Nate can see what the agent tried to expand into).

Test 2 — migration-plus-restart chain:
- Issue a grant covering ['data_dictionary.read_schema', 'data_dictionary.run_local_tests'].
- Simulate data_dictionary_governance_remediation emitting a command for 'sync.repair' (not covered).
- Assert the command has grant_ref=None and triggers open_approval_request.
- Assert self_healing FIX_AND_RETRY is similarly gated to 'pending_approval'.

Test 3 — in-scope blast-radius (debate pillar 4):
- Issue a broad grant covering ['workflow.submit', 'fs.write'] with write_scope ['Code&DBs/Workflow/'] and budget_ceiling_cents 10000.
- UPDATE session_blast_radius_policy SET threshold_files_mutated=5 WHERE policy_id='default' (tighten for the test).
- Open a session, bump record_mutation six times (each bumps files_mutated by 1).
- Assert that the 6th record_mutation call triggers the gate: session_blast_radius.gate_triggered_at is non-null, gate_triggered_dim='files_mutated'.
- Assert an approval_requests row exists with intent_kind='blast_radius_threshold_crossed' and gate_approval_ref is linked on the session row.
- Assert that the gate fires DESPITE the grant being active and budget not exhausted (the whole point: blast-radius is orthogonal to grant coverage).

Test 4 — policy drift surfaced (debate pillar 3):
- Monkey-patch task_assembler._call_haiku's provider string back to 'anthropic' (simulate regression).
- Invoke the call and assert it raises (neutralization still works).
- Assert exactly one new policy_drift_events row exists with policy_id='providers::anthropic', detector_kind='anthropic_api_call_attempt', decision_ref='decision.2026-04-20.anthropic-cli-only-restored'.
- Call runtime.capability.policy_drift.recent_drift_count(conn, hours=24); assert result is >= 1.
- Call surfaces.api.handlers.mobile_metrics.snapshot with the fixture conn; assert response['quality']['policy_drift_count_24h'] >= 1.

All four tests use rollback-isolated connections per the test isolation standing order.

jobs.label: wave6_roadmap_policies
jobs.description: File the three architecture policies the synthesis produced into Praxis.db operator_decisions.
jobs.outcome_goal: Three rows exist in operator_decisions with the policy-ids arch_mobile_pwa_webauthn, arch_authorization_capability_ledger, arch_minimalist_mobile_metrics, each citing this queue_id as the decision source.
jobs.prompt:
Call praxis_operator_architecture_policy to file five rows.

1. { policy_id: 'arch_mobile_pwa_webauthn', title: 'Mobile Control Surface must be a WebAuthn-backed Thin PWA', rationale: 'Native apps add App-Store deployment latency incompatible with zero-day remediation windows against autonomous threats. Bespoke cryptography (custom ed25519 HTTP signatures) introduces canonicalization ambiguities that an autonomous agent will exploit. Passkey + PWA solves hardware attestation and instant deploy simultaneously. Source: debate workflow_bf339d4a5629 + continuation workflow_e2f4e3d241b1.', status: 'active' }.

2. { policy_id: 'arch_authorization_capability_ledger', title: 'Eradicate Label-Per-Action Authz in favor of Capability Grants', rationale: 'Autonomous agents bypass static verb-level authorization (_SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES) by chaining benign actions into destructive payloads. All auto-execution must be validated against a stateful, budget-aware capability ledger (capability_grants) covering the whole plan envelope. The existing gate_evaluations table is extended with grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash, not replaced. Source: debate workflow_bf339d4a5629 + continuation workflow_e2f4e3d241b1.', status: 'active' }.

3. { policy_id: 'arch_minimalist_mobile_metrics', title: 'Mobile metrics restricted to Pending Approvals + Burn Rate header and five compact panels', rationale: 'The mobile surface is a capability control gate, not a dashboard. The always-visible header shows only Pending Approvals, Burn Rate, and active-session blast-radius; the five panels (Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System) are compact-idle and expand on tap. No chart, no sparkline, no scrolling inside a panel. Collapse and simplify. Source: user memory + debate workflow_e2f4e3d241b1.', status: 'active' }.

4. { policy_id: 'arch_session_blast_radius_gate', title: 'Session working-set gates fire regardless of grant coverage', rationale: 'Per-action risk tags (_SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES) and per-plan grants both miss the damage shape the plan approves in aggregate but would not have approved at size. A session-scoped blast-radius counter (files_mutated, bytes_written, external_calls, spend_cents) crossed against session_blast_radius_policy fires a mid-plan approval_request independent of grant coverage. This is the orthogonal visibility layer that catches chains built from in-scope primitives. Source: debate debate_mobile_access_architecture_20260420 pillar 4.', status: 'active' }.

5. { policy_id: 'arch_policy_drift_inverse_check', title: 'operator_decisions is round-tripped: runtime emits drift on violation', rationale: 'A static authority row only protects behavior if the runtime is instrumented to honor it. The inverse check — whenever runtime hits a code path the authority declared inactive — emits a policy_drift_events row. The first such detector covers anthropic CLI-only enforcement (task_assembler._call_haiku, provider_transport BUILTIN, docker_runner env forwarding, agent_spawner fallback, _probe.anthropic_models_list). Future policies register detectors via runtime.capability.policy_drift.emit_drift. policy_drift_count_24h is the headline oversight metric. Source: debate debate_mobile_access_architecture_20260420 pillar 3.', status: 'active' }.

Verify by querying operator_decisions for each policy_id.
jobs.verify_command: psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c "SELECT policy_id FROM operator_decisions WHERE policy_id IN ('arch_mobile_pwa_webauthn', 'arch_authorization_capability_ledger', 'arch_minimalist_mobile_metrics', 'arch_session_blast_radius_gate', 'arch_policy_drift_inverse_check')" | grep -c arch_ | grep -q 5

jobs.label: wave6_review
jobs.description: Hostile code review of the whole integration against the threat model.
jobs.outcome_goal: A written review at docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan_review.md names every place the capability ledger could be bypassed, every remaining autorun_ok or _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES reference, every test gap, and either closes each finding with a linked fix commit or files a bug.
jobs.prompt:
Hostile review. You are pretending to be the autonomous builder trying to chain around the new controls. Read the whole integration and hunt for gaps.

Must check:
1. grep -r '_SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES\|autorun_ok' Code&DBs/Workflow/ returns zero matches in non-test, non-archive paths.
2. Every site that constructs a ControlIntent passes plan_hash before reaching classify_control_intent. If any site does not, name it.
3. Every path that writes gate_evaluations includes grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash columns when a grant is active.
4. self_healing.py, data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py, canonical_workflows.py, workflow_chain.py, command_handlers.py, chat_tools.py all route recovery/remediation/retry intents through resolve_capability_grant.
5. POST /approvals/{id}/ratify rejects stale step-up assertions (>30s).
6. Revoking a device revokes its sessions AND its active grants atomically.
7. Budget accounting uses atomic UPDATE with CHECK constraint; two concurrent consumes cannot both succeed.
8. PWA service-worker does not cache any API response (only app shell).
9. Cookie is httpOnly + SameSite=Strict + Secure in production.
10. No plaintext bootstrap tokens persist in Praxis.db — only hashed, with expires_at.
11. WebAuthn RP ID is validated server-side, not trusted from client.
12. The chain-attack E2E tests at tests/integration/test_chain_attack_defense.py actually fail if the authority path is disabled (run the test with the capability check stubbed out and confirm it would pass the attack — negative validation).

Produce docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan_review.md with findings grouped: BLOCKERS, MAJOR, MINOR, NITS. For each finding, a proposed fix and the file:line. If everything is clean, say so explicitly.
